text.title: C# / ASP.NET Core Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Does a controller access DbContext directly?

conclusion.verdict: introduce a service layer; controller calls service

branch.question: Is DbContext registered as singleton or transient?
Does an async method lack CancellationToken?

conclusion.verdict: add CancellationToken parameter; pass to EF Core calls

branch.question: Are raw exceptions returned to clients?
Does a service return a tracked EF entity?

conclusion.verdict: map to record DTO at the boundary; never return navigation properties

branch.question: Is DateTime.UtcNow called directly in business logic?

conclusion.verdict: inject TimeProvider; use FakeTimeProvider in tests

branch.question: Are integration tests using EF InMemory?
Are multi-step writes lacking an explicit transaction?

conclusion.verdict: signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
